name: Recon
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``Recon`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: python ReconPy.py (then choose menu option 1 "WiFi Recon")

examples.command: python ReconPy.py (then choose menu option 1 "WiFi Recon")

dependencies: python3 stdlib only (socket, subprocess, os, re, platform, concurrent.futures)
system binaries: ping, arp / ip neighbor
algorithms: concurrent /24 ping sweep (ThreadPoolExecutor, 10 workers, IPs .1-.254) to populate ARP cache
ARP/neighbor-table parse via subprocess + regex (arp -a on Windows/macOS, ip neighbor on Linux)
produces: stdout list of responsive IPs during sweep
stdout list of (IP Address, MAC Address) pairs from the local ARP/neighbor table
novelty: none — thin wrapper. It does no 802.11/RF recon at all: no monitor mode, no airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools/scapy, no packet capture, no probe/PMKID/handshake work. It is purely a ping-sweep + ARP-table reader around OS builtins (ping, arp/ip neighbor), equivalent to `nmap -sn` or `arp -a`. Despite the name "WiFi Recon", the only WiFi aspect is that the host happens to be on a WiFi network; the tool sees no wireless-layer data.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
